revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:弁慶まつり]]
]]]]]]]]]]]]]] == Benkei Festival == [[File:Benkeimatsuri engeki.jpg|thumb|220px|The 28th Benkei Festival Play - The Legend of Benkei]] The '''Benkei Festival''' (弁慶まつり) is a {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|qid=Q132241|lt=Matsuri|1=Festival}} held annually on the first Friday and Saturday of October in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|qid=Q304266|lt=Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Tanabe, Wakayama}}. == Overview == The "Benkei Festival" was established in 1987 (Showa 62) following the recommendations of the Tanabe City Tourism Vision Implementation Planning Committee. It is based on the legend that {{ill|Benkei|en|Benkei|qid=Q316898|lt=Musashibō Benkei|1=Benkei}} was the son of the {{ill|Kumano Bettō|ja|熊野別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Bettō|1=Kumano bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568874}}, who governed the Tanabe area. The festival began as the "800th Anniversary of Musashibō Benkei and the Kumano Navy's Departure."<ref>[http://www.agara.co.jp/column/mizu/?i=321972 "30 Years of the Benkei Festival"], Kii Minpou</ref> The first person to play the role of Benkei was baseball commentator {{ill|Takenori Emoto|en|Takenori Emoto|qid=Q11551146|lt=Emoto Takenori|1=Takenori Emoto}}, while the role of Tanzao was played by Tanabe's then-mayor, {{ill|Ikoma Keizo|ja|生駒啓三|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Keizo|1=Keizō Ikoma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q100454790}}. About 150 participants took part in the Musashibō Benkei Kumano Navy Departure Parade. Held annually on the first Friday and Saturday of October, the festival features various events such as theatrical performances depicting the Legend of Benkei, the {{ill|Benkei Geta Dance|ja|弁慶ゲタ踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkei Geta Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oniwaka Taiko Drumming|ja|鬼若太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oniwaka Taiko Drumming|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, along with the Kishu Benkei Yosakoi Dance and a local product tent market. == Role of Musashibō Benkei == [[File:Benkei08.jpg|thumb|150px|8th Benkei Actor, {{ill|Izumi Kiyoshi|ja|泉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Kiyoshi|1=Kiyoshi Izumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30926779}}, the first citizen to play Benkei]] [[File:Benkei28.jpg|thumb|150px|28th Benkei Actor, {{ill|Yoshimoto Ryuhei|ja|吉本竜兵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimoto Ryuhei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] The first Benkei role was played by {{ill|Takenori Emoto|en|Takenori Emoto|qid=Q11551146|lt=Emoto Takenori|1=Takenori Emoto}}, followed by {{ill|Konishi Hiroyuki|ja|小西博之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konishi Hiroyuki|1=Hiroyuki Konishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11463575}} (2nd), {{ill|Minakata Eiji|ja|南方英二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakata Eiji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to チャンバラトリオ|1=Minakata Eiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11318243}} (3rd), and {{ill|Antonio Inoki|en|Antonio Inoki|qid=Q45207|lt=Antonio Inoki|1=Antonio Inoki}} (4th and 5th). The 6th Benkei was {{ill|Ryuko Koetsu|ja|龍虎孝悦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuko Koetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the 7th was {{ill|Kurama Ryuya|ja|蔵間龍也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Ryuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵間竜也|1=Kurama Tatsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6445554}}. The 8th Benkei, {{ill|Izumi Kiyoshi|ja|泉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Kiyoshi|1=Kiyoshi Izumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30926779}}, marked the first time an ordinary citizen played the role. Since then, candidates have been selected through public auditions. == Theatrical Performance - The Legend of Benkei == [[File:15代目弁慶役.jpg|thumb|150px|15th Benkei Actor, {{ill|Maruyama Masayuki|ja|丸山雅之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Masayuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first citizen actor in the play]] A special stage is set up at {{ill|Tōkei Shrine|ja|闘鶏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬪雞神社}} for the theatrical performance, which reenacts Musashibō Benkei’s life—from his birth, his encounter with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qid=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}, meeting Tamamushi, participating in the {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|qid=Q319771|lt=cockfighting ritual|1=Cockfighting}} to divine the outcome of battles between the Genji and Heike clans, and leading the {{ill|Kumano Navy|ja|熊野水軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Navy|1=Kumano Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568926}} into battle. All actors are amateur citizens chosen through auditions, who undergo about two months of training before the main performance. Until the 14th festival, there was no play; instead, there was a parade called the "Musashibō Benkei Kumano Navy Departure Procession." The first play debuted during the 15th festival, with {{ill|Maruyama Masayuki|ja|丸山雅之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Masayuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the first citizen actor to perform as Benkei on stage. == Benkei Geta Dance == [[File:Benkei geta.jpg|thumb|220px|Citizens enjoying the Geta Dance at the Benkei Festival]] The "Benkei Geta Dance" has been a staple of the festival since its first iteration. Participants march down the main street from {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|en|Kii-Tanabe Station|qid=Q2888653|lt=Kii-Tanabe Station|1=Kii-Tanabe Station}} to {{ill|Ogigahama Beach|ja|扇ヶ浜海水浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogigahama Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, performing choreography inspired by Musashibō Benkei and chanting, "Sorya, Un-tokosho! Arya, Dokkoisho!!"<ref>[http://www.kishu-benkei.com/geta/index.html Benkei Festival Official Site - Benkei Geta Dance]</ref> About 1,000 people participate annually. == Kishu Benkei Yosakoi Dance == Introduced at the 12th festival (1998, Heisei 10), the {{ill|Kishu Benkei Yosakoi Dance|ja|紀州弁慶よさこい踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Benkei Yosakoi Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.kishu-benkei.com/yosakoi/index.html Benkei Festival Official Site - Kishu Benkei Yosakoi Dance]</ref> features preliminary competitions held at different venues, including Ogigahama Beach, Beachside, Tōkei Shrine, and Aoi Street. The winning teams from these preliminaries compete in the finals at Ogigahama Beach to determine the champion. The person playing Benkei that year serves as one of the judges.
== See Also == * {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|qid=Q304266|lt=Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Tanabe, Wakayama}} * {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|en|Kii-Tanabe Station|qid=Q2888653|lt=Kii-Tanabe Station|1=Kii-Tanabe Station}} * {{ill|Tōkei Shrine|ja|闘鶏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬪雞神社}} * {{ill|Benkei|en|Benkei|qid=Q316898|lt=Musashibō Benkei|1=Benkei}} * {{ill|Kumano Bettō|ja|熊野別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Bettō|1=Kumano bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568874}} * {{ill|Kumano Navy|ja|熊野水軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Navy|1=Kumano Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568926}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kishu-benkei.com/ Official Benkei Festival Website] * [http://www.tanabe-kanko.jp/tanabeshi/jinbutsu/benkei/ Tanabe Tourism Association - "Explore Tanabe"]
